# Migration files whose deletion is intentional and reviewed.
#
# The check-deleted-migrations CI job (hogli lint:migration-deletions) blocks deleting any
# Django migration that exists on master: doing so diverges fresh databases from
# deployed ones and false-flags phantom migrations on other open PRs. See
# docs/published/handbook/engineering/safe-django-migrations.md.
#
# Add a path here ONLY when the deletion is genuinely safe and reviewed — e.g.
# relocating a product's migrations (git mv as part of a SeparateDatabaseAndState
# move), reverting a not-yet-deployed migration, or a squash. One entry per line.
# An entry ending in "/" allowlists every migration under that directory. Prefer
# removing stale entries once the acknowledging PR has merged.
#
# Examples (commented out — these are not active entries):
#   products/old_product/backend/migrations/   # whole-product move
#   posthog/migrations/0500_oops.py            # single revert
